phases.name: Phase 0 — Project Skeleton
phases.description: Repo scaffold, Makefile, golangci-lint, CI workflow, TDD scaffolding, CLAUDE.md, tasks.json bootstrap.
phases.deliverable: task version returns JSON success; make ci green on remote; CLAUDE.md authored; tasks.json validated.

tasks.title: Repo skeleton (go.mod + directory tree per §12.6)
acceptanceCriteria: go.mod sets module github.com/mandarnilange/corvee, go 1.23 (1.23 is required because the spec §12.1 plans to use range-over-function iterators in adapter/eventlog).
All packages from §12.6 exist with a one-line doc.go each.
go build ./... exits 0.

tasks.title: Makefile per §12.7 with all targets
acceptanceCriteria: Targets: build, build-all, test, test-unit, test-race, test-e2e, test-coverage, lint, ci, install-tools, hooks-install, tasks-validate, clean, release.
make help prints the target list.
make ci is the single entry point used by both local pre-push and GitHub Actions.
Coverage script enforces ≥90% domain / ≥85% usecase, skipping packages with no tests so Phase 0 stays green.

tasks.title: .golangci.yml with depguard clean-architecture enforcement
acceptanceCriteria: Linters enabled per §11.5: errcheck, govet, staticcheck, revive, unused, gosec, nilerr, exhaustive, depguard, ineffassign, misspell, unconvert, prealloc, bodyclose.
depguard rule: internal/domain forbids importing internal/{usecase,adapter,cli}.
depguard rule: internal/usecase forbids importing internal/{adapter,cli}.
depguard rule: internal/adapter forbids importing internal/{usecase,cli}.
Banned packages enforced: logrus, zap, testify, ginkgo, yaml.v2, yaml.v3 (per §12.5).
revive enforces godoc on every exported identifier.

tasks.title: GitHub Actions CI workflow
acceptanceCriteria: Runs make ci on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest with Go 1.23.
Triggers on push to any branch and PRs to main.
Caches go modules.
permissions: contents: read; no untrusted-input interpolation in run: commands.
Uploads coverage.out as an artifact for inspection.

tasks.title: task version verb (TDD-first; CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: test/e2e/version_test.go is written first and fails before any cmd/corvee code exists.
task version prints {"ok":true,"data":{"version":"<v>"}} to stdout and exits 0.
task --pretty version emits indented JSON (contains newlines).
stderr is empty at default verbosity.
internal/cli/root.go maps every domain sentinel to its §15.1 exit code, with a unit-test table covering each.
internal/cli/printjson.go is unit-tested for compact, pretty, and text modes.

tasks.title: Pre-push git hook + make hooks-install
acceptanceCriteria: .githooks/pre-push runs make ci and aborts the push on failure.
make hooks-install symlinks .githooks/* into .git/hooks/ and chmod +x's the source files.
Documented in CLAUDE.md §11.

tasks.title: CLAUDE.md authoring (working conventions for the codebase)
acceptanceCriteria: Sections cover: project intro, build/test, TDD-mandatory loop (verbatim §11.1), clean-architecture rules (§2.1), naming (§11.2 + §14.7), error→exit-code map (§14.3 + §15.1), spec ambiguity protocol (§14.8), phases pointer to .spec/tasks.json, banned dependencies (§12.5), commit format (no co-author tags), pre-push hook + background-validation parallelism (test-runner + lint + clean-architecture-validator + coderabbit).
All references to spec sections cite numbered §s so future readers can locate the source quickly.

tasks.title: docs/spec-questions.md with §16.1 Node-vs-Go resolution (SQ-001)
acceptanceCriteria: File exists with the §14.8 protocol header.
First entry SQ-001 records: §16.1 shows Node skill folder; §2/§12 mandate Go; resolution = Go is authoritative; §16.1 to be patched in a follow-up doc PR (tracked as TTR-E08-S06).

tasks.title: README.md + LICENSE (MIT) + .gitignore + .gitattributes
acceptanceCriteria: README links to .spec/corvee-spec.md as the source of truth and to CLAUDE.md for conventions.
LICENSE is MIT (per §12.7).
.gitignore covers bin/, dist/, coverage.out, .tasks/index.json, .tasks/locks/, .tasks/operations/, AND .tasks/local.json (per-VM agent identity; see SQ-003).
.gitattributes pins LF endings on text files.

tasks.title: .spec/tasks.json with all 100 stories enumerated + tasks-validate
acceptanceCriteria: tasks.json contains all 9 phases (TTR-E00..TTR-E08) with story counts 10/19/13/15/12/8/7/10/6 = 100.
Every story carries id, title, type, kind, priority, estimatedHours, files, required_capabilities, dependencies, acceptanceCriteria; none of those are empty (acceptanceCriteria has ≥1 entry).
Every dependency reference resolves to an existing story id within the document.
Every critical_path entry resolves to an existing story id.
cmd/tasks-validate/main.go enforces all of the above and is wired into make ci via make tasks-validate.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Core MVP
phases.description: Item domain (struct, validation, status enum), ID parsing/generation, Store interface, fsstore adapter, usecase + cli for init, add, list, show, update, done, delete (soft).
phases.deliverable: Single-user backlog: init, add a project/epic/story/subtask, list, show, update, done, soft-delete all work end-to-end. Coverage thresholds active (≥90% domain / ≥85% usecase).

tasks.title: domain.Item struct + ItemType/Kind/Priority/Risk/Role enums + tests (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Item struct exactly matches §14.2 fields and JSON tags.
Item round-trips through json.Marshal/Unmarshal byte-for-byte using a fixture from §5.
Required fields (schema_version, id, type, title, status, created_at, updated_at, version) round-trip even when optional fields are absent.
ItemType, Kind, Priority, Risk, and Role constants match §14.2 exactly; IsValid methods return false for unknown values; table-driven tests cover each enum value plus an unknown-value rejection.
Combined test coverage for item.go + types.go ≥90% (counts toward domain threshold).

tasks.title: domain.Status enum + transition validation
acceptanceCriteria: Status constants per §14.2: backlog, ready, claimed, in_progress, review, blocked, done, abandoned.

ValidTransition(from, to Status) returns true only for legal transitions (backlog→ready, ready→claimed, claimed→in_progress|ready, in_progress→review|blocked|done, review→done|in_progress, blocked→ready|in_progress, any→abandoned).
Table-driven test covers every legal pair plus a sample of illegal pairs.
Returns ErrInvalidStatus for illegal transitions.

tasks.title: domain.ID parser/generator (per-parent monotonic numbering)
acceptanceCriteria: ParseID handles all forms from §4: PROJECT, PROJECT-E01, PROJECT-E01-S03, PROJECT-E01-S03-T02.
ChildID(parent, n) returns the next free child ID under parent given an iterable of existing siblings.
ParentOf returns the parent ID or empty string for projects.
ProjectCode is 3–6 uppercase letters; rejects invalid forms with ErrUsage.
Includes a property test asserting parse(generate(x))==x for any valid x and parentOf(childId(p,n))==p.

tasks.title: Split config: domain.Workspace (synced) + domain.Local (gitignored) + adapter/config loaders
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace struct (committed at .tasks/workspace.json): schema_version, workspace_name, default_project, claim_ttl_minutes, heartbeat_interval_minutes, critical_path, git.{auto_commit, auto_sync_seconds}. Stable across VMs; safe to git sync.

Local struct (gitignored at .tasks/local.json): default_agent, agent_role, agent_capabilities. Per-VM, never synced. This split resolves SQ-003 — see docs/spec-questions.md — and intentionally diverges from spec §5.1's single config.json design to avoid the multi-VM merge-conflict trap.
domain.WorkspaceLoader port (Load/Save) and domain.LocalLoader port (Load/Save) are separate so usecases can request only what they need.

Env-var overrides per §7.1 / §18: CORVEE_AGENT_ID overrides Local.default_agent; CORVEE_AGENT_ROLE overrides Local.agent_role; CORVEE_AGENT_CAPABILITIES (CSV) overrides Local.agent_capabilities. Resolved in adapter/config.LoadLocalEffective.

Loading a missing local.json returns sensible defaults (agent_role=human, default_agent=$USER or empty, empty capabilities). Loading a missing workspace.json after task init has run is an integrity error.
Schema validation rejects unknown fields in either file with ErrSchemaInvalid.
Tests cover: load/save round-trip for each file, missing-file defaults, invalid-schema rejection, env-var override precedence (env > local.json > built-in default).

tasks.title: JSON Schema (embed.FS) + gojsonschema validator
acceptanceCriteria: JSON Schema (draft-07) for Item committed under internal/domain/schema/.
Schema embedded via embed.FS so the binary has no runtime asset deps.
Validate(item) returns nil for the §5 fixture and ErrSchemaInvalid (wrapped) with field-path detail for mutated invalid versions.

Forward-version guard: items whose schema_version is GREATER than the binary's CurrentSchemaVersion are rejected with a wrapped ErrSchemaInvalid carrying a clear message ("item written with schema_version=N; this binary supports up to M; please upgrade the task CLI"). This prevents the multi-VM poisoning case where Agent A on a newer binary writes schema_version=2 and Agent B on an older binary reads it.
Tests assert each required field's absence triggers a validation error AND that schema_version > current produces the upgrade-prompt error.

tasks.title: domain.Store port + ListFilter type
acceptanceCriteria: Store interface exactly per §14.1: Get, List, Put (with expectVersion), Delete, Exists.
ListFilter struct exactly per §14.2.
Godoc on every exported method per CLAUDE.md §5.

tasks.title: testfakes.Store implementing domain.Store (in-memory)
acceptanceCriteria: FakeStore stores items in a sync.Mutex-protected map.
Put persists exactly the Item the caller supplied — version, updated_at, and all other fields. The store does NOT mutate Version or UpdatedAt; usecases own time and version intent.
Put with expectVersion >= 0 compares against the on-disk version and returns ErrVersionConflict on mismatch without writing.
Get returns ErrNotFound for unknown ids.
List honors every ListFilter field.
Self-tests cover every behavior so usecase tests can rely on the fake.

tasks.title: fsstore atomic write (tmp + fsync + rename) (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Write path: <id>.json.tmp.<pid>.<ts> → fsync → POSIX rename to <id>.json (§6 Layer 1).
store_test.go covers: write-then-read round-trip; reading a missing item returns ErrNotFound; tmp files left on disk after a simulated crash do not appear under Get/List.
Filenames use only the canonical id (no path traversal possible).

tasks.title: fsstore version-aware Put with ErrVersionConflict (single-writer)
acceptanceCriteria: Put re-reads the on-disk file just before the atomic rename; aborts with ErrVersionConflict if expectVersion >= 0 and the disk version differs from expectVersion. Read+rename ordering plus POSIX rename atomicity is sufficient for the single-writer model Phase 1 targets; cross-process per-item locking arrives in Phase 3 (TTR-E03-S04) when concurrent agents become possible.
Phase 1's deliverable is single-writer; the docstring and CHANGELOG note this explicitly so users do not assume multi-agent safety yet.
Put persists the Item the caller supplied verbatim — Version and UpdatedAt are set by the usecase before Put is invoked. fsstore never imports a Clock.
Tests cover the conflict path, the success path, and the no-expectation path (expectVersion = -1).

tasks.title: usecase.Init (bootstrap .tasks/ + workspace.json + local.json + .gitignore)
acceptanceCriteria: Creates .tasks/{items,events,operations,locks,hooks} and writes .tasks/.gitignore excluding index.json, operations/, locks/, AND local.json (§3, §6, SQ-003).
Writes workspace.json (committed) with workspace_name, default_project (if --project given), claim_ttl_minutes=60, heartbeat_interval_minutes=30, empty critical_path, git defaults.
Writes local.json (gitignored) with default_agent (from --agent flag, $CORVEE_AGENT_ID env, or $USER), agent_role=human, empty agent_capabilities.
Idempotent: re-running with the same args is a no-op (preserves existing local.json so the per-VM identity isn't clobbered).
Returns ErrUsage when run inside an existing workspace unless --force is set.

tasks.title: usecase.Add (id assignment + parent/type validation)
acceptanceCriteria: Validates parent type per §15.2: epic→project, story→epic, subtask→story.
Computes the next sibling number under the parent and assembles the new ID via ChildID.
Usecase sets Item.CreatedAt = Item.UpdatedAt = clock.Now() and Item.Version = 1 before calling Store.Put with expectVersion = -1 (new item). The store does not mutate these fields.
Returns ErrUsage on invalid type/parent combinations and ErrNotFound when parent is absent.

tasks.title: usecase.List (filter application via Store.List)
acceptanceCriteria: All ListFilter fields from §14.2 are honored.
Empty filter returns every item.
Sort and Order flags supported (priority|created_at|updated_at|due_date).

tasks.title: usecase.Show (single item lookup)
acceptanceCriteria: Returns the full item including journal.
ErrNotFound for unknown ids.

tasks.title: usecase.Update with --expect-version
acceptanceCriteria: Mutators per §15.2: status, priority, title, description, due, add-tag, remove-tag, add-impact-file, remove-impact-file, add-dep, remove-dep, add-acceptance, remove-acceptance, risk.
Status mutations call domain.ValidTransition; illegal transitions return ErrInvalidStatus.
Usecase sets Item.UpdatedAt = clock.Now() and Item.Version = old.Version + 1 before calling Store.Put. ExpectVersion threads through; conflict returns ErrVersionConflict (no retry — caller decides).
Returns prev_version and new_version in the output struct.

tasks.title: usecase.Done (status=done, completed_at=now)
acceptanceCriteria: Usecase sets Item.Status = done, Item.CompletedAt = clock.Now(), Item.UpdatedAt = clock.Now(), Item.Version = old.Version + 1 before calling Store.Put. The store never touches time or version.
Optional --note appends a journal entry with event=completed (entry timestamp also from the injected clock).
Lease enforcement deferred to Phase 3; Phase 1 ignores --lease-id.

tasks.title: usecase.Delete (soft only — sets status=abandoned)
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 1 only implements soft-delete (status=abandoned).
--hard, --cascade, --force flagged as ErrUsage in Phase 1; full impl arrives in Phase 4.
Refuses if any other item lists this id in dependencies/blocks (returns ErrIntegrityViolated).

tasks.title: cli wiring for init/add/list/show/update/done/delete
acceptanceCriteria: Each verb follows the §14.5 pattern: one file, one new<Verb>Cmd(deps), flags via cmd.Flags(), RunE returning errors that root.go maps to exit codes.
Output uses printJSON; --pretty/--text honored.
No business logic in cli/* — only flag parsing and a single usecase call.

tasks.title: cmd/corvee/main.go DI wiring (Store, Clock, IDGen)
acceptanceCriteria: main.go wires concrete adapters: fsstore.New for Store, real clock for Clock; IDGen stub returns deterministic strings (real ULID arrives in Phase 3).
cli.Deps now embeds the usecase.Deps; verbs pull only what they need.
Workspace auto-discovery walks up from cwd looking for .tasks/ (per §18 recommendation).

tasks.title: Phase 1 E2E: init→add×3→list→show→update→done (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a temp workspace via task init.
Adds a project, epic, and story; asserts IDs follow §4 (RKN, RKN-E01, RKN-E01-S01).
Lists with filters, shows by id, updates priority, marks done.
All output is well-formed JSON; all exit codes are 0.
make ci is green at this point — coverage thresholds active and met.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Hierarchy & Validation
phases.description: tree, validate, reindex, summary, clone; parent/child integrity rules; dependency cycle detection; sharded events log + index cache.
phases.deliverable: Full project/epic/story/subtask navigation; dependency validation; fast list via index; sharded audit log persisted.

tasks.title: domain.Graph (id→Item, parent→children, id→deps)
acceptanceCriteria: Graph type with three Maps from §6 (id→Item, parent_id→child_ids, id→dependencies).
BuildGraph(items []Item) returns a populated Graph in O(N).
Children(id), Dependencies(id), Dependents(id), Descendants(id) helpers each O(1) lookup.

tasks.title: Dependency cycle detection + property test
acceptanceCriteria: DetectCycle(graph) returns ([]string of involved ids, true) on cycles, (nil, false) otherwise.
Algorithm is iterative DFS with a recursion-stack set.
Property test (rapid): a generated DAG never has cycles; the same DAG with one inserted back-edge always does.

tasks.title: Parent/type integrity validator
acceptanceCriteria: ValidateParentType enforces: project has no parent; epic→project; story→epic; subtask→story.
ValidateNoOrphans confirms every parent_id resolves.
ValidateAliasResolution confirms aliases[] do not collide with active ids.
Returns []IntegrityError listing every violation rather than failing on the first.

tasks.title: domain.EventLog port + Event type
acceptanceCriteria: EventLog interface per §14.1: Append, Read, Tail.
Event struct per §14.2: ts, actor, id, event, from, to, op_id, lease_id, metadata.
EventFilter per §14.2.

tasks.title: adapter/eventlog (sharded JSONL append + read)
acceptanceCriteria: Daily-sharded files: .tasks/events/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl (§6 Layer 5).
Append uses O_APPEND with line-atomic writes (one event per line, ≤4KiB).
Read iterates shards in date order, applies filter, sorts within shard by ts.
EventFilter.IDPrefix matches both the exact id and descendants (RKN-E01 matches RKN-E01-S03).

tasks.title: domain.Indexer port + Index type
acceptanceCriteria: Indexer interface per §14.1: Read, Rebuild, Invalidate.
Index struct per §14.2 includes BuiltAt, ItemCount, MaxMtime, ByID, ByStatus, ByAssignee, ByParent, Unblocked, DependencyGraph.

tasks.title: adapter/index — build + read + staleness detection
acceptanceCriteria: Builds Index from items/ + events.
Read compares item_count and max_mtime in header against current items/; rebuilds if stale (§6).
Index lives at .tasks/index.json and is written atomically (tmp+rename).
Index.json is gitignored (already in .gitignore).

tasks.title: usecase.Tree (hierarchical view)
acceptanceCriteria: Builds a tree from the index; honors --max-depth and --status filters.
Each node carries its full item plus children: [].
Empty workspace yields { roots: [] } with exit 0.

tasks.title: usecase.Validate (workspace integrity check)
acceptanceCriteria: Runs all checks from §15.2: schema per item, parent existence and type, no cycles, no orphaned aliases, index consistency, no orphaned op files (Phase 4), clock skew within tolerance (Phase 3).
Output lists every error in errors[] but exit 0 even when valid=false.

tasks.title: usecase.Reindex (rebuild index.json)
acceptanceCriteria: Forces Indexer.Rebuild and reports items_indexed, events_replayed, shards_read, was_stale.
Idempotent: running twice yields was_stale=false the second time.

tasks.title: usecase.Summary (rollups + critical_path + deliverables)
acceptanceCriteria: Output exactly per §15.2: totals, by_<field>, critical_path (with next field), deliverables.
Group-by --by field: type|status|kind|priority|assignee|project|epic.
Reads from the index, not from items/, so the call is cheap.

tasks.title: usecase.Clone (no children variant)
acceptanceCriteria: Clones with fresh ID under original parent (or --to).
Resets status=backlog, claim=nil, claimed_at=null, completed_at=null, journal=[{created}], version=1.
Children are not cloned in Phase 2; --with-children returns ErrUsage (handled in Phase 4).

tasks.title: Phase 2 cli wiring + E2E
acceptanceCriteria: All five verbs follow the §14.5 cli pattern.
E2E test creates a multi-level workspace, runs tree/validate/reindex/summary, asserts shapes from §15.2.
make ci green; coverage thresholds met.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Distributed-safe Concurrency
phases.description: Lease IDs (ULIDs), claim/release/heartbeat/done with --lease-id, reap, next with capability matching, idempotency via --operation-id, per-item Locker, full concurrency test suite.
phases.deliverable: Multiple agents on the same machine can safely share a backlog. v0.3 shippable.

tasks.title: adapter/idgen ULID generator + testfakes.SeqIDGen
acceptanceCriteria: Real impl uses oklog/ulid/v2 with a monotonic entropy source.
FakeIDGen returns IDs from an injected sequence so tests are deterministic.
Generated ULIDs are sortable and length 26.

tasks.title: adapter/clock real impl + testfakes.FixedClock
acceptanceCriteria: Real Now() returns time.Now().UTC().
FixedClock allows tests to advance time deterministically (Advance(d Duration)).

tasks.title: domain.Lease validity logic + tests
acceptanceCriteria: IsExpired(claim, now, skew) compares now > expires_at + skew.
Default skew tolerance is 5 minutes per §6 Layer 3.
MatchesLeaseID(claim, leaseID) — guard for §14.3 ErrLeaseMismatch.
Tests cover boundary cases including exact-tie equality.

tasks.title: domain.Locker port + adapter/fsstore O_EXCL impl (cross-process serialization for Phase 3+)
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 1 shipped single-writer fsstore (TTR-E01-S09). This story adds the per-item lock that makes concurrent writers safe — Locker.Acquire blocks until the lock is obtained or ctx is cancelled (§14.1).

fsstore impl uses os.OpenFile(path, O_RDWR|O_CREATE|O_EXCL, 0o600) on .tasks/locks/<id>.lock; release deletes the file. Stale locks (older than a configurable threshold) are reaped on acquire attempt.
Returns ErrLockTimeout per §14.3 when ctx deadlines.

fsstore.Put is updated to always acquire the per-item lock for the read-modify-write cycle once Phase 3 ships. There is no config toggle; locking is part of the correctness contract for concurrent agents.
Phase 1's Phase-1-style tests still pass with locking enabled (single-writer path is a degenerate case).

Platform scope: workspace MUST live on a local POSIX filesystem (linux/{amd64,arm64}, darwin/arm64). NFS, SMB, and other network filesystems are explicitly unsupported because O_EXCL is unreliable on them (well-known kernel races). task validate uses statfs to detect non-local filesystem types and emits an integrity warning when the workspace is hosted on one. Windows is out of scope by §12.7 (build-all does not target it).

tasks.title: usecase.Claim (atomic + lease ID issuance) (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Acquires per-item lock; reads item; rejects with ErrAlreadyClaimed if claim already set; writes claim object (agent, lease_id from IDGen, claimed_at, expires_at) and status=claimed via Store.Put with expectVersion.
Returns ClaimOutput{Item, LeaseID} per §14.4.
Tests cover success path, already-claimed path, status-not-claimable path.

tasks.title: usecase.Release with --lease-id check
acceptanceCriteria: Requires --lease-id; ErrLeaseMismatch on mismatch (exit 5).
Clears claim, reverts status to ready unless explicitly changed.

tasks.title: usecase.Heartbeat with --lease-id check
acceptanceCriteria: Refreshes expires_at to now+TTL.
ErrLeaseMismatch on lease ID mismatch.
Tests assert heartbeat does not change version unnecessarily (only updates expires_at).

tasks.title: usecase.Done extension with --lease-id check
acceptanceCriteria: When the item is currently claimed, --lease-id is required and validated.
--force allows stealing the close (logs to events).
Phase 1 tests still pass (backward compatibility for unclaimed items).

tasks.title: usecase.Reap (TTL-based release with skew tolerance)
acceptanceCriteria: Iterates claimed items; releases those past expires_at + skew (§6 Layer 3).
Idempotent.
Reports reaped: [{ id, was_assigned_to, lease_id, expired_at }] per §15.2.
Auto-invoked inside task next per §6.

tasks.title: usecase.Next (filters + critical-path bonus + tie-breakers + --auto-claim)
acceptanceCriteria: Loads workspace.json via WorkspaceLoader (TTR-E01-S04) for critical_path; loads local.json (with env-var overrides) via LocalLoader for default_agent and agent_capabilities; --match-capabilities filters to items whose required_capabilities ⊆ effective capabilities.
Selection per §15.2: filter, drop unmet deps, critical-path bonus, sort by priority desc → critical-path order asc → due_date asc → updated_at asc → id asc.
--max-hours filters by estimated_hours.
--affinity boosts items where agent appears in past journal actor.
--auto-claim atomically claims the chosen item; race losers see ErrAlreadyClaimed and re-pick on retry.

Sort comparator is unit-tested with a permutation matrix that exercises every tie-breaker independently: nil due_date vs set due_date (nils sort last), equal priority forcing critical-path-order resolution, missing critical-path entry, equal updated_at forcing id resolution, and the all-equal case (deterministic id order). The test asserts no panic and a stable, totally-ordered result for ≥50 generated permutations.

tasks.title: Operation ID idempotency (op-result store + replay)
acceptanceCriteria: domain.OpResultStore port: Get(opID) (raw json, ok, err), Put(opID, payload) error. Adapter persists results at .tasks/operations/results/<op-id>.json (gitignored), atomic write via tmp+rename, max payload size 1 MiB.

Every mutating verb (add, update, claim, release, heartbeat, done, journal, move, rename, delete, clone, import) wraps its work in WithIdempotency(opID, fn) which: (a) returns the persisted result if Get(opID) succeeds, (b) runs fn and persists fn's result via Put(opID, ...) on success.
Events.jsonl still records op_id for audit (§6 Layer 7) but is no longer the source of replay payloads — the op-result store is.
Auto-mints op_id when absent and surfaces it on the response so retries can pass it back.
TTL: op-result entries older than 24h are pruned by `task compact` (Phase 8); the table notes this so the dir doesn't grow unboundedly.
Tests cover: first call writes a result and returns it; second call with same op_id returns the persisted result without mutating state; result for a missing op_id returns Get's not-found path.

tasks.title: store_concurrent_test.go — N-goroutine race tests under -race -count=10 (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: N goroutines update the same item; final on-disk version is strictly monotonic.
No torn JSON observable mid-flight.
All tests pass with go test -race -count=10.

tasks.title: claim contention concurrent test (1000 iterations × N goroutines)
acceptanceCriteria: 1000 iterations × N goroutines call Claim on the same item; exactly one succeeds per iteration; others receive ErrAlreadyClaimed.
All under -race -count=10.

tasks.title: lease validation concurrent test
acceptanceCriteria: Goroutine A holds a valid lease; goroutine B does heartbeat with a wrong lease ID; B is rejected with ErrLeaseMismatch and A's lease is unchanged.

tasks.title: Phase 3 cli wiring (claim/release/heartbeat/reap/next) + E2E
acceptanceCriteria: E2E spawns 10 child processes calling task next --auto-claim against 5 ready items.
Exactly 5 succeed (exit 0 with full payload); exactly 5 receive null payload or exit 5.
make ci green; v0.3 shippable.

phases.name: Phase 4 — Multi-file Ops & Recovery
phases.description: Operation journal (WAL) for move/rename/delete-cascade/clone-with-children; task recover; task migrate; crash-injection harness.
phases.deliverable: Multi-file ops are crash-safe; schema migrations supported.

tasks.title: domain.Operation + OpStep types per §14.2
acceptanceCriteria: Operation struct: op_id, kind, agent, started_at, status, plan ([]OpStep) per §14.2.
OpStep struct: step (int), kind, args (map[string]any), done (bool).
JSON round-trip tests.

tasks.title: domain.OpLog port
acceptanceCriteria: OpLog interface per §14.1: Begin (returns op_id), MarkStep, Complete, Pending.

tasks.title: adapter/oplog (Begin/MarkStep/Complete/Pending)
acceptanceCriteria: Operation files at .tasks/operations/<op-id>.json (gitignored).
Begin writes intent atomically; MarkStep updates done flags; Complete flips status to completed and schedules grace-period deletion.
Pending returns operations in pending|executing state.

tasks.title: usecase.Move (cascading new IDs + alias bookkeeping + ref rewriting)
acceptanceCriteria: Procedure per §8.2: compute new IDs, mv files, update parent_id/project_id, append old IDs to aliases[], scan all items for old IDs in dependencies/blocks and rewrite, append moved event with full mapping, bump version on every touched item.
Atomic at the workspace level via OpLog: either all changes apply or none do.
Tests assert mapping correctness on a 3-deep hierarchy.

tasks.title: usecase.Rename (explicit target ID)
acceptanceCriteria: Same machinery as Move but takes an explicit target ID.
Refuses if target already exists (returns wrapped error → exit 1).
Cascades to children when their IDs depend on the renamed parent.

tasks.title: usecase.Delete --cascade (with dependent guard)
acceptanceCriteria: --cascade includes children atomically via OpLog.
--hard removes the JSON files (git history retains them).
Refuses without --force when any other item lists this id in dependencies/blocks (ErrIntegrityViolated).

tasks.title: usecase.Clone --with-children
acceptanceCriteria: --with-children cascades the clone via OpLog.
Output includes { root, descendants } per §15.2.

tasks.title: usecase.Recover (roll forward orphaned ops) (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Reads OpLog.Pending; for each, resumes from the next undone step (§6 Layer 6).
Auto-invoked by task list/task next when an in-flight op is detected.
Failed recovery surfaces in task validate.
Output: { recovered: [{ op_id, kind, steps_completed, steps_skipped }], failed: [...] }.

tasks.title: usecase.Migrate (schema_version upgrades)
acceptanceCriteria: Detects items with schema_version < current; applies registered upgraders.
Idempotent.
Auto-invoked when task list/task show encounters an old item.

Items with schema_version > current are reported in skipped[] with reason="binary too old" — they are NOT migrated, NOT downgraded, and NOT silently dropped. This is the symmetric counterpart to the forward-version guard in TTR-E01-S05 and prevents a newer-binary write from poisoning an older-binary workspace.
Returns { migrated: [{ id, from_version, to_version }], skipped: [{ id, current_version, binary_supports, reason }] }.

tasks.title: Crash-injection test harness (panic mid-step)
acceptanceCriteria: Test harness panics after writing an intent and after marking each step done.
Restart + Recover yields a consistent state in every injection point (§11.3 Phase 4 requirement).
Runs under -race -count=10.

tasks.title: Move/Rename idempotency property test
acceptanceCriteria: rapid generates random valid hierarchies; move/rename + recover yields the same state regardless of crash injection.
Aliases preserve old-id resolvability.

tasks.title: Phase 4 cli wiring + E2E with crash recovery
acceptanceCriteria: E2E kills the process during task move; restart; task recover rolls forward; task validate reports clean.
make ci green.

phases.name: Phase 5 — Real-time Observability
phases.description: task watch event stream with filtering; structured --metadata JSON on done/journal; relative --since parser.
phases.deliverable: Reactive planner agents possible; structured handoff data round-trips. v0.5 shippable.

tasks.title: adapter/watch — fsnotify-based event tail
acceptanceCriteria: Watches the events shard directory; follows the current day's file.

Rolls into tomorrow's shard at midnight: when the injected Clock crosses the day boundary, the watcher closes the old file handle, opens the new shard, and resumes streaming without dropping any concurrently-appended event.
Closes the channel on ctx.Done() (§14.1).

tasks.title: domain.EventFilter matcher (multi-flag AND, CSV value OR)
acceptanceCriteria: Matches event, actor, id, from, to, op_id with AND between flags and OR within CSV values.
--id RKN-E01 matches descendants RKN-E01-S03 and RKN-E01-S03-T02.
Table-driven tests cover every field plus the prefix-id case.

tasks.title: usecase.Watch (filtering + --since + --exit-on)
acceptanceCriteria: Replays from --since up to live, then streams.
Exits on --limit or --exit-on event types (§15.2).
Returns 130 on Ctrl+C (SIGINT).

tasks.title: usecase.Journal extension with --metadata + event types
acceptanceCriteria: --event values: note (default), progress, blocker, decision, handoff (§15.2).
--metadata accepts arbitrary JSON object (or --metadata-file path).
--lease-id required when --event ∈ {progress, blocker, decision} and the item is claimed.

tasks.title: usecase.Done extension with --metadata
acceptanceCriteria: --metadata JSON attached to the journal entry.
Schema is unconstrained per §5; tool stores and surfaces, never validates keys.
Tests cover --metadata and --metadata-file.

tasks.title: cli/watch with --format jsonl|text|compact
acceptanceCriteria: Emits one event per line (no top-level envelope) per §15.2.
Format conversions covered by table-driven tests.

tasks.title: Watch integration test (cross-goroutine event detection)
acceptanceCriteria: Goroutine A appends an event; goroutine B running task watch picks it up within a deadline.
Filter correctness validated end-to-end.

Midnight-rotation regression test: drives the FixedClock across a day boundary while goroutine A appends events to both the closing shard (just before midnight) and the opening shard (just after); asserts every event is delivered exactly once to the watcher in correct order. Run under -race -count=10 so the fsnotify rename/create race surfaces if it regresses.

tasks.title: --since relative parser (5m, 1h, today, yesterday, ISO timestamp)
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts ISO timestamps (RFC 3339), durations (5m, 1h, 24h), and the literals today/yesterday.
Returns ErrUsage on unparsable input.
Table-driven tests cover every accepted form plus invalid samples.

phases.name: Phase 6 — HTML Render & Deploy Handoff
phases.description: Static HTML dashboard (board / tree / summary) with default + dark themes, deploy manifest for vercel-skill handoff. Templates and CSS embedded via embed.FS.
phases.deliverable: task render writes .tasks/dist/*.html plus manifest.json; HTML diffs match snapshot fixtures byte-for-byte.

tasks.title: adapter/render templates (board.html.tmpl + tree.html.tmpl + summary.html.tmpl)
acceptanceCriteria: Board: kanban columns by status, cards grouped by epic, color-coded by priority.
Tree: collapsible project → epic → story → subtask with status pills + completion percentages.
Summary: per-project progress bars, completion velocity from events, blocked-task callouts, agent activity feed.
All views read-only and client-rendered with vanilla JS.

tasks.title: adapter/render themes (default.css + dark.css)
acceptanceCriteria: Two themes cover the §9 visual contract.
--theme flag selects between them; falls back to default on unknown name.

tasks.title: embed.FS for templates + themes
acceptanceCriteria: All templates and themes embedded via embed.FS — no runtime asset deps (§12.4).
Test asserts the embedded FS contains every expected file.

tasks.title: usecase.Render orchestrates via domain.Renderer port (no direct file writes)
acceptanceCriteria: domain.Renderer port: Render(ctx, RenderInput{Items, Events, Theme, OutDir}) (RenderOutput{Files, Stats}, error). All file writes (index.html, tree.html, summary.html, assets/styles.css, assets/app.js per §17.2) live in adapter/render — usecase.Render never touches the filesystem.
usecase.Render only orchestrates: load items via Store, load events via EventLog, validate theme, call Renderer.Render, return its output.
Reads items + events only; never mutates.
Returns { out_dir, files, manifest, stats } per §15.2.
Tests for usecase.Render use a fake Renderer; tests for adapter/render use a t.TempDir() output and assert files appear with expected names + non-empty bodies.

tasks.title: manifest.json generator per §17.2
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest contains: name, version (git SHA + ts), generated_at, entrypoint, static, framework, build_command, output_directory, routes (§17.2).
version falls back to 0.0.0-dev-<ts> when git is unavailable.
Schema validated by a small Go check.

tasks.title: HTML snapshot tests against fixture workspaces
acceptanceCriteria: Render a known fixture workspace; diff each .html against the committed snapshot.
go test -update regenerates snapshots; CI rejects drift.
Snapshots are stable across runs (no timestamps embedded in HTML).

tasks.title: cli/render with --out and --theme
acceptanceCriteria: Default --out is .tasks/dist/.
--theme name validated against embedded themes; unknown name returns ErrUsage.

phases.name: Phase 7 — Git Sync & Distributed Merge
phases.description: task sync, task merge with deterministic claim-conflict resolution (Layer 8), 3-way merge for status/journal/tags, task import and export, auto-sync background loop.
phases.deliverable: Cross-machine distribution. Multi-VM ready. v1.0 shippable.

tasks.title: adapter/git (os/exec wrapper for pull/push/add/commit/mv)
acceptanceCriteria: Wraps git via os/exec; surface includes Pull --rebase, Push, Add, Commit, Mv, ConflictedFiles.
Returns structured errors (e.g. ErrConflict) so usecase can branch.
Tests run against a t.TempDir() git repo (real git binary required).

tasks.title: usecase.Sync (pull --rebase + push) (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Pulls with --rebase, then pushes.
On conflict, dispatches to usecase.Merge for per-task resolution.
Configurable via config.json:git.auto_commit.
auto_sync_seconds is not consumed by one-shot sync; it is read only by task sync --watch (TTR-E07-S08).

tasks.title: usecase.Merge with deterministic claim-conflict resolution (lower lease_id wins)
acceptanceCriteria: Per-task JSON conflicts resolved deterministically.
Claim conflicts: lower lease_id wins (§6 Layer 8); the loser sees ErrClaimLost on next operation.
Records claim_lost event in events shard.

tasks.title: 3-way merge for status/journal/tags + prompt for prose fields
acceptanceCriteria: status: latest (max version) wins.
journal: union by ts+actor+event triple, dedupe.
tags: union, dedupe.
description / acceptance_criteria: surface for human review (writes a .conflict file).

tasks.title: usecase.Import (native + phases-and-tasks formats)
acceptanceCriteria: Both formats per §15.2: native (array of full items) and phases-and-tasks (AgentForge-style).

Phases-and-tasks accepts the §15.2 minimum field set (id, title, description, type, priority, estimatedHours, files, acceptanceCriteria) AND the §5 native Item fields used by the dogfood tracker (kind, required_capabilities, dependencies, status, completed_at). Each maps directly to its Item counterpart when the task becomes a story; no field outside §5 is permitted.
Top-level critical_path on the import doc populates config.json:critical_path on the target workspace (§5.1).
--dry-run validates and reports without writing.
Atomic via OpLog: nothing written if any item invalid (returns ErrSchemaInvalid).
Self-test: imports .spec/tasks.json (the dogfood case) into a temp workspace and asserts 100 stories appear with their status, completed_at, dependencies, and required_capabilities preserved.

tasks.title: usecase.ExportPlan (native, phases, markdown formats)
acceptanceCriteria: Three formats per §15.2: native, phases, markdown.
--out writes to a path; default stdout.
Round-trip self-test: import → export native → import yields the same item set.

tasks.title: usecase.Export (graph / cypher / dot)
acceptanceCriteria: graph: JSON Graph Format (nodes + edges with metadata).
cypher: Neo4j load script.
dot: Graphviz.
--include filters edge types: parent, dependency, blocks, alias.

tasks.title: task sync --watch (explicit long-running sync loop)
acceptanceCriteria: task sync without --watch is a one-shot pull/push and exits (default behavior already from TTR-E07-S02).

task sync --watch is an explicit long-running command that loops every config.json:git.auto_sync_seconds (or the --interval override) until ctx is cancelled (Ctrl+C). Exits 0 on clean shutdown, 130 on SIGINT.
Rejected as ErrUsage if config.json:git.auto_sync_seconds is null AND --interval is not given.
Tested via context cancellation: the loop exits cleanly within one tick of cancellation.
Avoids the conceptual flaw of trying to run a background goroutine inside a one-shot CLI invocation that would otherwise exit immediately.

tasks.title: Two-repo + bare middle E2E (planner-executor) (CRITICAL PATH)
acceptanceCriteria: Two temp repos pointed at a third bare repo.
Planner imports 20 tasks; pushes.
Executor pulls; claims, completes 20 items.
Planner pulls and sees 20 done.
make ci green; v1.0 cut.

tasks.title: Distributed claim-conflict E2E (lower lease_id wins)
acceptanceCriteria: Two executors claim the same item from divergent branches; sync triggers merge.
Lower lease_id wins; loser sees claim_lost: true on next operation.
Both repos reach identical state after final sync.

phases.name: Phase 8 — Plugins, Hooks, Compaction, SKILL.md
phases.description: Plugin protocol via os/exec + JSON over stdio; shell hooks; agent_safe enforcement; task compact; SKILL.md and references/ docs.
phases.deliverable: Extensibility surfaces live; v1.x maintenance command shipped; skill is publishable.

tasks.title: Plugin protocol (external binary + JSON over stdin/stdout)
acceptanceCriteria: Plugins are external binaries under plugins/<name>/; CLI invokes via os/exec, sends event JSON on stdin, reads JSON on stdout.
Errors from plugins are logged but don't fail core operations unless the plugin returns a 'veto' field.
Sample plugin in docs/plugin-protocol.md shows a Python-based auto-tagger.

tasks.title: Plugin discovery (plugins/ directory)
acceptanceCriteria: Walks plugins/<name>/plugin.json on startup; registers handlers per declared event type.
Malformed plugin.json is logged and skipped (does not break the CLI).

tasks.title: Shell hook emission (.tasks/hooks/*.sh)
acceptanceCriteria: On each lifecycle event, run the matching shell script under .tasks/hooks/ if executable.
Event JSON piped on stdin; non-zero exit logged but ignored.
Sample on-status-change.sh in test fixtures.

tasks.title: agent_safe enforcement under CORVEE_AGENT_MODE
acceptanceCriteria: CORVEE_AGENT_MODE=1 or --agent flag triggers agent-safe filtering (§10).
Plugins without agent_safe: true are skipped silently in agent mode.
Tests cover both modes.

tasks.title: usecase.Compact (squash events, archive shards, prune ops, gc aliases)
acceptanceCriteria: Default cutoff: 90 days.
Squashes events into item state; archives or deletes old daily shards; prunes resolved op files; gc's stale aliases.
Idempotent.
Returns { shards_archived, ops_pruned, aliases_pruned, bytes_saved } per §15.2.

tasks.title: SKILL.md + references/ (workflows, schema, error-codes, topologies, handoff-patterns) + §16.1 patch
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md frontmatter exactly per §16.2; body sections per §16.3.
Five reference files cover §16.4 contents.
Spec §16.1 patched from Node-based to Go-based skill folder layout (resolves SQ-001 in docs/spec-questions.md).
Skill is publishable as a release asset.
